members.name: swarm-s1
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Analyze ClaudeCode competitor

**Task ID:** s1
**Title:** Analyze ClaudeCode competitor

Read the source code located at ../claude-code-sourcemap. Identify its core features, unique capabilities, UI/UX patterns, and any gaps compared to our product. Summarize findings in a concise bullet list highlighting features that could be adopted or improved.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task.

IMPORTANT: Your VERY FIRST action must be a tool call. Call `team_read_messages` with team_name set to the team name from your context. Do NOT respond with text first — call the tool immediately.

After reading messages, do the work described above using tool calls (glob, read, bash, etc.). When done, call `team_task_complete` to mark task "s1" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

members.name: swarm-s5
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Create SWARMOUT2.md with feature suggestions and implementation plan

**Task ID:** s5
**Title:** Create SWARMOUT2.md with feature suggestions and implementation plan

Combine the summaries from s1‑s4 to propose new features for our product. For each suggested feature, define detailed Milestones and tasks suitable for implementation (including descriptions, acceptance criteria, and rough effort estimates). Write the document as SWARMOUT2.md and place it in target/temp/swarmout. Ensure the file includes sections: Competitor Summaries, Feature Additions, Milestones, and Tasks.

The Milestones should be ordered logically (e.g., Research, Prototype, Development, Testing, Release). Each task should be atomic and clearly actionable.

Finally, create any necessary placeholder files or directories in target/temp/swarmout so that the output path exists.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task.

IMPORTANT: Your VERY FIRST action must be a tool call. Call `team_read_messages` with team_name set to the team name from your context. Do NOT respond with text first — call the tool immediately.

After reading messages, do the work described above using tool calls (glob, read, bash, etc.). When done, call `team_task_complete` to mark task "s5" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.
